folio.label: 153v
folio.l: By twixt vs two , nedetú no straunge fare
Fare wel Cosyn , god shilde ©ow from care
And if that any thyngö , by day or nygút
If it be in my power , and my mygút
That ©e me wol comaunde , in any wyse
It shal be don , rigút as ©e wol deuyse
But oo thyngö or that ©e gon , if it may be
I wolde preye ©ow , for to lene me
An hondred Frankes , for a weke or tweye
For certeyne bestes , that I must beye
To store witú a place , that is oures
God helpe me so , I wolde it were ©oures
I shal nat faile surely , of my day
Nat for a thousand frankes , a myle way
But lat this thyngö besecree , I ©ow preye
For ©et to nygút , theise bestes mot I beye
And fare now wel , myn owen Cosyn deere
Graunt mäcy of ©oure cost , and of ©oure chere
¢ This noble Marchau~t , gentilly a noon
Answered and seide , o Cosyn myn , Daun Ioún
Now sekirly , this is a smal request
My gold is ©oures , whan that it ©ow lest
And nat oonly my gold , but my chaffare
Take what ©ow lest , god shilde that ©e spare
But o thyngö is this , ©e knowen it wel I now
Of chapmen , that here moneye is here plow
We moun creaunce , whil we han a name
But goldlees for to ben , it is no game
Paie it a geyn , whan it litú in ©oure ese
After my mygút , ful fayn wyl I ©ow plese
Theise hondred frankes , he fette fortú a noon
And péuely he toke hem , to Daun Ioún
No wigút in al this world , wist of this lone
Sauyngö this Marchau~t , and Daun Ioún allone
They drynke and speke , & rome a while and pleye
Til that Daun Ioún , ridetú to his Abbeye
¢ The morwe cam , and fortú this Marchau~t ridetú
To Flaundres ward , his Prentys wel hiö gydetú
Til he come in to Brugges , merily
Now gotú this Marchau~t , faste and bisily
A boute hise nedes , and byetú and creauncetú
He neither pleyetú atte dees , ne dauncetú
But as a Marchau~t , shortly for to telle
He ledetú his lyf , and there I lete hiö dwelle
